stem: A ISP analysis study measures the download speed (Mbps) for a sample of 40 subscribers. The sample mean is 117.2 Mbps with a sample standard deviation of 31.2 Mbps. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 117.2 Mbps. 95% CI = [107.2, 127.2]. Point estimate: 117.2 Mbps.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
